comments: Approved. The amendment explicitly replaces the prior hardcoded 6-stage lifecycle assumption with a YAML-based DAG workflow engine: it states the old paradigm was rigid dispatch.json progression and the new paradigm ships a YAML parser with workflows under .agent-state/workflows/*.yaml supporting dependencies, conditional logic, and looping (tasks/v0.8-kickoff-amendment.md:14-18), satisfying review criteria 1. It explicitly adds the LLM Workflow Router, stating that the Coordinator uses an LLM to evaluate available YAML workflows and dynamically select the most appropriate one from natural-language intent (tasks/v0.8-kickoff-amendment.md:19-22), satisfying review criterion 2. It explicitly requires porting and adapting Archon’s 17 default workflows to the heterogeneous JSON-contract-based federation model with L2/L3 Gatekeeper governance (tasks/v0.8-kickoff-amendment.md:23-25), satisfying review criterion 3. It also explicitly adds custom workflow authoring support (tasks/v0.8-kickoff-amendment.md:26-27), satisfying review criterion 4. The revised milestone map accommodates the amendment across M1-M6: M1 covers .agent-state migration plus the core YAML DAG parser; M2 covers the LLM router and registry; M3 is dedicated to porting the Archon 17 workflows; M4-M6 continue the downstream automation, custom authoring/team sync, and legacy deprecation sequence needed to absorb the new architecture (tasks/v0.8-milestone-map-v2.md:5-33), satisfying review criterion 5. Criterion 6 also passes: the directive marks actual coding and active model changes as out of scope for this amendment phase (tasks/v0.8-kickoff-amendment-directive.json:3-9), and the current git workspace is clean with no observed modifications to src/mcp-server/src/index.ts, workflow/dispatch.json, or other implementation files.
